title: gilia squarrosa
intro: Gilia squarrosa, also known as Downy Gilia or Sticky Gilia, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Gilia squarrosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a popular herbal remedy among those interested in natural healing practices.
